mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in nonfarm payrolls over the prior three months signals rising aggregate labor income that drives consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following top-quintile 3-month PAYEMS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.0% signals sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the mean return in those periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of accelerating consumer price inflation and a rising federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening that sharply compresses equity valuations through higher real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to show a negative marginal effect on 20-day returns (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, accelerating industrial production signals demand is running into capacity constraints, prompting margin compression from input costs and tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration are not negative in late-cycle periods, with positive return frequency exceeding 50% or mean return not below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts exceeding 10% over three months signals weakening residential investment and an impending slowdown in construction-related employment and consumer durables spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) after 10% HOUST drops or positive return frequency exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery, a quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP signals durable demand rebuilding rather than inventory restocking noise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth accelerates > 1pp from prior quarter
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive following GDPC1 acceleration in the two years after a recession end (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month moving average, it signals a labor market turning point that precedes broad earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds its 12-month average by 0.3pp, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and retail sales confirms a broad-based contraction in goods supply and consumer demand, amplifying recession probabilities and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-RSAFS interaction term fails to significantly predict negative 5-day returns (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window, or joint-decline episodes show positive returns more than 50% of the time.
